blocks.content: From the start, it was a nightmare.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is this the effect of Dream Star?
It wasn't easy for Yahweh's mind to be filled with anxiety, likely due to the brain's chemical reactions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No matter what, this is a bit much...
As he stood up, countless corpses formed a wall approaching from the horizon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yahweh, do you know those who died because of you? Do you know those you couldn't save?
Shirone's guilt.
Behind the ideal of saving humanity, there were people dying right now.
All life ends in death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The words Fermi had said before taking Dream Star seemed to linger in his subconscious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Feel our pain, our resentment!
Even as the corpses approached with hostility, Shirone didn't move.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This kind of dream.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He wanted to alleviate his guilt by being torn apart by them.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What are you doing?
Fermi appeared among the rotting corpses, looking around Shirone's dream with interest.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hmm, I thought you were just a stern old man, but you have a surprisingly soft side?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't talk nonsense. This is your doing. What kind of drug did you give me?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Even if you say that, it's the next-generation Dream Star improved by the best drug designer. Fear and Eros have clear purposes, making address matching easy.
As soon as Shirone recognized Fermi's words, the forms of the corpses became more concrete.
Although they didn't seem blurry before, everything was a mental effect.
Uuuu… Uuuuu!
Between their tattered clothes, glimpses of chests and genitals could be seen.
As Shirone made a disgusted face, Fermi shrugged as if he understood.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: When two things mix, this is what happens.
Shirone looked away.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So… what do I do now? Under Coder is outside the boundary of Dreamo.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Nothing. Just wait until Dream Star is fully absorbed in your stomach. When your brain is half-awake, the dream will collapse, and then you go to Under Coder.
It was a simple method, but the waiting place being a nightmare was the problem.
The corpses, now close enough to show their teeth, were waiting for an opportunity.
The reason they couldn't attack immediately was that Shirone's mind was blocking the progression of events.
Blocking the progression of a dream was something even a pro diver couldn't do.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You've changed a lot, that freshman brat.
During magic school.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We fought a lot. Back then, you were so rustic, but now you're the Messiah who will save humanity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't want to talk about that with you.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: There's nothing else to do, right? In a dream, the concept of time is all mixed up. If you immerse yourself, it goes faster than reality, but like this, who knows how long you'll wait.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It reminds me of the senior survival test. Trapped in ice water, cut by blades… Did we reach the 7th stage? Oh, I withdrew at the 6th stage.
Rrrr!
The corpses took a step closer.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Honestly, I thought you'd die at the 7th stage. If I knew you'd pass, I would've followed through.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you trying to say?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Just… I feel a bit resentful? Anyway, I'm a loser, but this is a dream.
Fermi extended his hand.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Maybe we can turn things around.
Raaaah!
The escape route was in the air, but he would be intercepted by Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rage.
The corpses caught in the explosion of light were torn apart, and pieces of flesh flew tens of meters.
In the open view, Fermi grabbed a chip made from a depreciation deal and put it in his mouth.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Let's enjoy this.
Regulation-style - Unmanned Sniper.
In the next moment, hundreds of bullets fired from 374 places in the air aimed at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's called a railgun.
The speed of the bullets accelerated through electromagnetic fields was something even an archmage couldn't react to.
Ugh!
As the Hand of God swept Shirone up, the bullets struck the ground.
The shockwave shook the earth.
Raaaah!
In the place where the corpses' flesh fell like hail, Shirone stood unscathed.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Hmm, something I haven't seen before? A kind of… mental technique?
Looking up at the giant hand of light floating in the air, Fermi smiled.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: His eyes were friendly.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You deceive your mind like a law. It's a good ability. But it won't work twice.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Feeling the genuine killing intent, Shirone responded sincerely.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shall we try?
As the Hand of God spread its palm towards the sky, a giant photon cannon formed.
High-speed bullets and a powerful cannon.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Even in the imminent situation, Fermi calmly observed the Hand of God.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: He used scale to transcend the limits of power. It's typical of Shirone, but…
He turned to face forward again.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: If it were me, I would've developed the trick further. No matter how strong the power, there's always an escape route. On the other hand, that technique can subdue the enemy 100% if the situation fits. It weakens on the second use, but that's the charm of a trick, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What does it matter what magic I research? Mind your own magic.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Haha, true.
As Fermi turned away, the ground shook, and the scenery began to crack.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The dream was collapsing.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Time flies when you're immersed. It seems the drug is taking effect now. Let's get going.
Shirone deactivated the Hand of God but couldn't shake off his unpleasant feelings.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So you picked a fight on purpose?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Well, sort of.
He was a man whose true intentions were impossible to read.
The two, now light in the Under Coder, followed a blue line to the middle layer.
In a room filled with numerous mirrors, Shirone asked, "Where are we going?"

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The Abyss. The graveyard of information and the world's final destination. It's also called the Apocalypse. Sounds familiar?
Shirone had heard that discarded information was restored to forge his signature.
After passing through the middle layer, they touched a mirror leading to the Abyss.
As the boundary reversed, the world of the Apocalypse rushed into Shirone's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is this…?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Why? Surprised because it's different from what you expected?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It certainly was.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Miro said the Apocalypse was a place that embodied a future world destroyed. But this…
The entire visible landscape was covered in an unknown mucous substance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that mucus?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yes. Since Nane left the photon system, the Apocalypse has been completely covered in mucus. According to investigations, over 98% of the surface is mucous.
Fermi pointed around.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: As you know, the Abyss is the graveyard of information. It's an end-of-the-world scene realized based on the real world. I think this predicts a cell buster in reality.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was quite surprised.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How do you know all this? Even Omega's logs are very old records.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: As time passes, even a king's secret diary ends up in bookstores. You're revealing things to me now, right? So in the future, many more people will know.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The future?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What an audacious thought.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yes. The Abyss endlessly accumulates the future. Therefore, the longer something exists here, the closer it is to our reality's near future. So wouldn't it be possible to predict events that haven't happened yet? I call this time mining.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone blinked.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since then, I've thoroughly investigated the Apocalypse. I've even picked up scraps of paper abandoned deep in the mountains. But those are still future information that hasn't happened in reality.
The irony of trash here becoming gold in reality.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I collected such information as much as possible to predict and prepare for future events. The emotional disease is one of them. Of course, your decision to make a deal with me came after hearing this explanation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I understand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt confident in his past judgment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I won't ask about the deal. But how did you collect so much information? Even if it's trash, finding near-future information must be difficult.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It was really tough. Trying to do it alone didn't work out. So I hired them—the Parrot Mercenaries.
Fermi blew his bangs away.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sister Marsha is here?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Smiling, Fermi pointed his thumb at the lowlands covered in mucus.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Want to go see?
Flying through the sky, they arrived at a city center where a giant excavator was set up.
Marsha stood next to it while mercenary leaders sat on metal boxes drinking.
Fermi walked over with his hands behind his back.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: How's it going?

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Huh? Fermi, what brings you here…

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Shirone! Guys! Come here! We have a celebrity!
The mercenary group now dealt with global matters, but Shirone's appearance still caused quite a stir.

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: …I didn't fight him directly.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: You! How did you get even cooler? A man needs power after all, right?
Marsha had a knack for making any conversation comfortable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You haven't changed. How have you been?

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Haha! I'm dying doing things not meant for me—detective work, tomb raiding, and now construction. Who knew singing about mining would lead to actual mining?
Marsha's face looked more haggard than before but her eyes sparkled like a child's.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Are you drilling through mucus with this machine?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. We're taking a break now but it's pointless anyway. Even if we drill through it regenerates.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: If even the tallest buildings disappear, it must be quite thick. Have you reached the bottom?

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: We hit solid ground early this morning at about 480 meters.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 480 meters?
Marsha tapped the excavator.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: It's piled up immensely. Even if we drill down exploration is impossible. More importantly…
The mucus underfoot wriggled and extended like tentacles wrapping around Marsha's leg.
The tip of the tentacle hardened like a needle and shot up just as Marsha roughly yanked it away.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: This sticky thing is driving me crazy. Ugh, typical—it knows how to be pretty, huh? This thing's definitely male.

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: Freeman approached.

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: It's because you're near the excavation machine. It seems to instinctively sense anything that threatens it.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Freeman.
Before Shirone could even properly greet him, Marsha tossed the tentacle onto the ground and snapped angrily.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Shut up. Then you should've been nearby. All you do is drink booze all day. You—curse.

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: Curse?

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: Her mouth's gotten so foul that the crew held a vote. Marsha's banned from swearing for now.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Hmph! You think that'll stop me? Curse, you little curse! I swear I'll just curse you into pieces!

blocks.speaker: Freeman
blocks.content: …Haha.
Shirone stared in bewilderment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: They sure live in an amusing way.
